represents a 3D position\ntype Point struct {\n\tX, Y, Z float64\n}\n\n// Space represents the room dimensions\ntype Space struct {\n\tWidth, Depth, Height float64\n}\n\n// Bounds returns the bounding box of the space\nfunc (s *Space) Bounds() (minX, minY, minZ, maxX, maxY, maxZ float64) {\n\treturn 0, 0, 0, s.Width, s.Depth, s.Height\n}\n\n// WallMaterial defines the type of wall material\ntype WallMaterial string\n\nconst (\n\tMaterialDrywall  WallMaterial = \"drywall\"\n\tMaterialBrick    WallMaterial = \"brick\"\n\tMaterialConcrete WallMaterial = \"concrete\"\n\tMaterialGlass    WallMaterial = \"glass\"\n\tMaterialMetal    WallMaterial = \"metal\"\n)\n\n// Wall represents a wall segment with material properties\ntype Wall struct {\n\tX1, Y1, X2, Y2 float64\n\tMaterial        WallMaterial\n\tAttenuation     float64 // dB loss\n}\n\n// Stats tracks simulation statistics\ntype Stats struct {\n\tFramesSent     atomic.Int64\n\tFramesPerSec   float64\n\tStartTime      time.Time\n\tLastStatsTime  time.Time\n\tLastFramesSent int64\n}\n\nfunc main() {\n\tflag.Var(&wallFlag{}, \"wall\", \"Add a wall as x1,y1,x2,y2 (can be repeated)\")\n\tflag.Parse()\n\n\tlog.SetFlags(log.Ldate | log.Ltime | log.Lmicroseconds)\n\tlog.Printf(\"[SIM] CSI Simulator CLI starting\")\n\n\t// Parse space dimensions\n\tspace, err := parseSpace(*flagSpace)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tlog.Fatalf(\"[SIM] Invalid space dimensions: %v\", err)\n\t}\n\n\t// Initialize random seed\n\tif *flagSeed == 0 {\n\t\t*flagSeed = time.Now().UnixNano()\n\t}\n\trng := rand.New(rand.NewSource(*flagSeed))\n\tlog.Printf(\"[SIM] Random seed: %d\", *flagSeed)\n\n\t// Validate channel\n\tif *flagChannel < 1 || *flagChannel > 14 {\n\t\tlog.Fatalf(\"[SIM] Invalid channel: %d (must be 1-14)\", *flagChannel)\n\t}\n\n\t// Create virtual nodes\n\tnodes := createVirtualNodes(*flagNodes, space, rng)\n\n\t// Create walkers (may be nil for node-to-node mode)\n\twalkers := createWalkers(*flagWalkers, space, rng)\n\n\t// For node-to-node mode, create walkers after nodes exist\n\tif WalkerType(*flagWalkerType) == WalkerTypeNodeToNode {\n\t\twalkers = createNodeToNodeWalkers(*flagWalkers, nodes)\n\t}\n\n\tlog.Printf(\"[SIM] Configuration:\")\n\tlog.Printf(\"[SIM]   Mothership: %s\", *flagMothership)\n\tlog.Printf(\"[SIM]   Scenario: %s\", *flagScenario)\n\tlog.Printf(\"[SIM]   Nodes: %d\", *flagNodes)\n\tlog.Printf(\"[SIM]   Walkers: %d\", *flagWalkers)\n\tlog.Printf(\"[SIM]   Rate: %d Hz\", *flagRate)\n\tlog.Printf(\"[SIM]   Duration: %d s\", *flagDuration)\n\tlog.Printf(\"[SIM]   Space: %.1fx%.1fx%.1f m\", space.Width, space.Depth, space.Height)\n\tlog.Printf(\"[SIM]   Walls: %d\", len(walls))\n\tlog.Printf(\"[SIM]   BLE: %v\", *flagBLE)\n\n\t// Output GDOP overlay if requested\n\tif *flagGDOPOverlay {\n\t\tif err := outputGDOPOverlay(space, nodes, *flagCellSize); err != nil {\n\t\t\tlog.Fatalf(\"[SIM] Failed to output GDOP overlay: %v\", err)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Output shopping list if requested\n\tif *flagShoppingList {\n\t\tif err := outputShoppingList(space, nodes); err != nil {\n\t\t\tlog.Fatalf(\"[SIM] Failed to output shopping list: %v\", err)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// If only output was requested, exit here\n\tif *flagGDOPOverlay || *flagShoppingList {\n\t\tif !*flagVerify && *flagDuration == 0 {\n\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[SIM] Output complete (no simulation requested)\")\n\t\t\tos.Exit(0)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Create scenario configuration\n\tscenarioConfig := &ScenarioConfig{\n\t\tType:      ScenarioType(*flagScenario),\n\t\tStartedAt: time.Now(),\n\t\tFallParams: FallScenarioParams{\n\t\t\tTriggerAfter:      *flagFallDelay,\n\t\t\tDescentDuration:   *flagFallDuration,\n\t\t\tStillnessDuration: *flagStillness,\n\t\t\tMinVelocity:       -1.5, // Below -1.5 m/s threshold\n\t\t\tMinZDrop:          0.8,  // At least 0.8m drop\n\t\t\tEndZ:              0.3,  // Floor level\n\t\t},\n\t\tOTAParams: OTAScenarioParams{\n\t\t\tUpdateAfter:      10 * time.Second,\n\t\t\tFirmwareSize:     *flagOTASize,\n\t\t\tNewVersion:       *flagOTAVersion,\n\t\t\tRebootDelay:      3 * time.Second,\n\t\t\tBootFailDuration: 30 * time.Second,\n\t\t\tSimulateFailure:  *flagOTAFailure,\n\t\t},\n\t}\n\n\t// Create context for shutdown\n\tctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(context.Background())\n\tdefer cancel()\n\n\t// Handle interrupt signal\n\tsigChan := make(chan os.Signal, 1)\n\tsignal.Notify(sigChan, os.Interrupt)\n\n\t// Open CSV writer if specified\n\tvar csvWriter *CSVWriter\n\tif *flagOutputCSV != \"\" {\n\t\tcsvWriter, err = NewCSVWriter(*flagOutputCSV)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\tlog.Fatalf(\"[SIM] Failed to open CSV file: %v\", err)\n\t\t}\n\t\tdefer func() { _ = csvWriter.Close() }() //nolint:errcheck\n\t\tlog.Printf(\"[SIM] Writing ground truth to %s\", *flagOutputCSV)\n\t}\n\n\t// Start statistics reporter\n\tstats := &Stats{StartTime: time.Now()}\n\tgo reportStats(ctx, stats)\n\n\t// Connect all nodes to mothership\n\tif err := connectNodes(ctx, nodes); err != nil {\n\t\tlog.Fatalf(\"[SIM] Failed to connect nodes: %v\", err)\n\t}\n\tdefer closeAllNodes(nodes)\n\n\t// Main simulation loop\n\tsimulationComplete := make(chan struct{})\n\tgo runSimulation(ctx, nodes, walkers, space, rng, csvWriter, stats, simulationComplete, scenarioConfig)\n\n\t// Wait for completion or interrupt\n\tvar durationTimer <-chan time.Time\n\tif *flagDuration > 0 {\n\t\tdurationTimer = time.After(time.Duration(*flagDuration) * time.Second)\n\t} else {\n\t\t// Never timeout if duration is 0\n\t\tdurationTimer = make(chan time.Time)\n\t}\n\n\tselect {\n\tcase <-simulationComplete:\n\t\tlog.Printf(\"[SIM] Simulation completed\")\n\tcase <-sigChan:\n\t\tlog.Printf(\"[SIM] Interrupted by user\")\n\t\tcancel()\n\tcase <-durationTimer:\n\t\tlog.Printf(\"[SIM] Duration elapsed\")\n\t\tcancel()\n\t}\n\n\t// Verify blob count if requested\n\tif *flagVerify {\n\t\tif err := verifyBlobs(*flagWalkers, walkers, space); err != nil {\n\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[SIM] Verification FAILED: %v\", err)\n\t\t\tos.Exit(1)\n\t\t}\n\t\tlog.Printf(\"[SIM] Verification PASSED\")\n\t}\n\n\t// Print final statistics\n\tprintFinalStats(stats, len(walkers))\n}\n\n// parseSpace parses space dimensions from WxDxH format\nfunc parseSpace(s string) (*Space, error) {\n\tparts := strings.Split(s, \"x\")\n\tif len(parts) != 3 {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"expected WxDxH format, got: %s\", s)\n\t}\n\twidth, err := strconv.ParseFloat(parts[0], 64)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"invalid width: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\tdepth, err := strconv.ParseFloat(parts[1], 64)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"invalid depth: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\theight, err := strconv.ParseFloat(parts[2], 64)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"invalid height: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\treturn &Space{Width: width, Depth: depth, Height: height}, nil\n}\n\n// createVirtualNodes creates virtual nodes positioned in the space\nfunc createVirtualNodes(count int, space *Space, rng *rand.Rand) []*VirtualNode {\n\tnodes := make([]*VirtualNode, count)\n\n\tfor i := 0; i < count; i++ {\n\t\tnode := &VirtualNode{\n\t\t\tID:   i,\n\t\t\tMAC:  generateMAC(i),\n\t\t\tRole: \"tx_rx\",\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Distribute nodes around perimeter\n\t\tperimeter := 2*(space.Width+space.Depth)\n\t\tpos := float64(i) / float64(count) * perimeter\n\n\t\tif pos < space.Width {\n\t\t\t// Bottom edge\n\t\t\tnode.Position = Point{X: pos, Y: 0, Z: 2.0}\n\t\t} else if pos < space.Width+space.Depth {\n\t\t\t// Right edge\n\t\t\tnode.Position = Point{X: space.Width, Y: pos - space.Width, Z: 2.0}\n\t\t} else if pos < 2*space.Width+space.Depth {\n\t\t\t// Top edge\n\t\t\tnode.Position = Point{X: space.Width - (pos - space.Width - space.Depth), Y: space.Depth, Z: 2.0}\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t// Left edge\n\t\t\tnode.Position = Point{X: 0, Y: space.Depth - (pos - 2*space.Width - space.Depth), Z: 2.0}\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tnodes[i] = node\n\t}\n\n\treturn nodes\n}\n\n// generateMAC generates a synthetic MAC address for a virtual node\nfunc generateMAC(id int) [6]byte {\n\tvar mac [6]byte\n\tmac[0] = 0xAA\n\tmac[1] = 0xBB\n\tmac[2] = 0xCC\n\tmac[3] = byte((id >> 16) & 0xFF)\n\tmac[4] = byte((id >> 8) & 0xFF)\n\tmac[5] = byte(id & 0xFF)\n\treturn mac\n}\n\n// createWalkers creates synthetic walkers\nfunc createWalkers(count int, space *Space, rng *rand.Rand) []*Walker {\n\twalkerType := WalkerType(*flagWalkerType)\n\n\tif walkerType == WalkerTypePathFollow {\n\t\treturn createPathWalkers(count, space)\n\t}\n\n\tif walkerType == WalkerTypeNodeToNode {\n\t\t// Will be created after nodes are initialized\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\n\t// Default: random walk\n\twalkers := make([]*Walker, count)\n\n\tfor i := 0; i < count; i++ {\n\t\twalkers[i] = &Walker{\n\t\t\tID:   i,\n\t\t\tType: WalkerTypeRandomWalk,\n\t\t\tPosition: Point{\n\t\t\t\tX: rng.Float64() * space.Width,\n\t\t\t\tY: rng.Float64() * space.Depth,\n\t\t\t\tZ: 1.7,\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t\tVelocity: Point{\n\t\t\t\tX: (rng.Float64() - 0.5) * 0.5,\n\t\t\t\tY: (rng.Float64() - 0.5) * 0.5,\n\t\t\t\tZ: 0,\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t\tSpeed:  0.8 + rng.Float64()*0.4,\n\t\t\tHeight: 1.7,\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn walkers\n}\n\n// createPathWalkers creates walkers that follow predefined paths\nfunc createPathWalkers(count int, space *Space) []*Walker {\n\tif *flagPathFile == \"\" {\n\t\tlog.Printf(\"[SIM] Path-following mode requires --path-file, using default rectangular path\")\n\t\treturn createDefaultPathWalkers(count, space)\n\t}\n\n\t// Load paths from JSON file\n\tpaths, err := loadPathsFromFile(*flagPathFile)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tlog.Fatalf(\"[SIM] Failed to load paths from %s: %v\", *flagPathFile, err)\n\t}\n\n\twalkers := make([]*Walker, count)\n\tfor i := 0; i < count; i++ {\n\t\t// Use path modulo count to allow fewer paths than walkers\n\t\tpathIdx := i % len(paths)\n\t\twalkers[i] = &Walker{\n\t\t\tID:      i,\n\t\t\tType:    WalkerTypePathFollow,\n\t\t\tPath:    paths[pathIdx],\n\t\t\tPathIdx: 0,\n\t\t\tSpeed:   1.0,\n\t\t\tHeight:  1.7,\n\t\t}\n\t\tif len(paths[pathIdx]) > 0 {\n\t\t\twalkers[i].Position = paths[pathIdx][0]\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn walkers\n}\n\n// createDefaultPathWalkers creates walkers following a default rectangular perimeter path\nfunc createDefaultPathWalkers(count int, space *Space) []*Walker {\n\tmargin := 0.5\n\tpath := []Point{\n\t\t{X: margin, Y: margin, Z: 1.7},\n\t\t{X: space.Width - margin, Y: margin, Z: 1.7},\n\t\t{X: space.Width - margin, Y: space.Depth - margin, Z: 1.7},\n\t\t{X: margin, Y: space.Depth - margin, Z: 1.7},\n\t}\n\n\twalkers := make([]*Walker, count)\n\tfor i := 0; i < count; i++ {\n\t\tstartIdx := (i * len(path) / count) % len(path)\n\t\twalkers[i] = &Walker{\n\t\t\tID:      i,\n\t\t\tType:    WalkerTypePathFollow,\n\t\t\tPath:    path,\n\t\t\tPathIdx: startIdx,\n\t\t\tSpeed:   0.8 + 0.4*float64(i)/float64(count),\n\t\t\tHeight:  1.7,\n\t\t}\n\t\tif len(path) > 0 {\n\t\t\twalkers[i].Position = path[startIdx]\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn walkers\n}\n\n// createNodeToNodeWalkers creates walkers that traverse between virtual nodes\nfunc createNodeToNodeWalkers(count int, nodes []*VirtualNode) []*Walker {\n\tif len(nodes) < 2 {\n\t\tlog.Fatalf(\"[SIM] Node-to-node mode requires at least 2 nodes\")\n\t}\n\n\twalkers := make([]*Walker, count)\n\tfor i := 0; i < count; i++ {\n\t\twalkers[i] = &Walker{\n\t\t\tID:      i,\n\t\t\tType:    WalkerTypeNodeToNode,\n\t\t\tNodes:   nodes,\n\t\t\tNodeIdx: 1, // Target is second node\n\t\t\tSpeed:   1.0,\n\t\t\tHeight:  1.7,\n\t\t}\n\t\t// Start at first node\n\t\tif len(nodes) > 0 {\n\t\t\twalkers[i].Position = nodes[0].Position\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn walkers\n}\n\n// PathDefinition defines a walker path in JSON format\ntype PathDefinition struct {\n\tWaypoints []Point `json:\"waypoints\"` // Ordered list of points to visit\n}\n\n// loadPathsFromFile loads walker paths from a JSON file\nfunc loadPathsFromFile(filename string) ([][]Point, error) {\n\tdata, err := os.ReadFile(filename)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, err\n\t}\n\n\tvar paths []PathDefinition\n\tif err := json.Unmarshal(data, &paths); err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, err\n\t}\n\n\tif len(paths) == 0 {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"no paths defined in file\")\n\t}\n\n\tresult := make([][]Point, len(paths))\n\tfor i, pathDef := range paths {\n\t\tif len(pathDef.Waypoints) == 0 {\n\t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"path %d has no waypoints\", i)\n\t\t}\n\t\tresult[i] = pathDef.Waypoints\n\t}\n\n\tlog.Printf(\"[SIM] Loaded %d paths from %s\", len(result), filename)\n\treturn result, nil\n}\n\n// connectNodes connects all virtual nodes to the mothership.\n// Each node gets its own persistent connection with background goroutines\n// for ping, health, and message reading.\nfunc connectNodes(ctx context.Context, nodes []*VirtualNode) error {\n\t// Get or generate token\n\ttoken := *flagToken\n\tif token == \"\" {\n\t\tvar err error\n\t\ttoken, err = provisionToken()\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"failed to provision token: %w\", err)\n\t\t}\n\t\tlog.Printf(\"[SIM] Auto-provisioned token: %s...\", token[:min(16, len(token))])\n\t}\n\n\t// Parse mothership URL\n\twsURL, err := url.Parse(*flagMothership)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"invalid mothership URL: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\t// Convert http(s) to ws(s)\n\tif wsURL.Scheme == \"http\" {\n\t\twsURL.Scheme = \"ws\"\n\t} else if wsURL.Scheme == \"https\" {\n\t\twsURL.Scheme = \"wss\"\n\t}\n\n\terrChan := make(chan error, len(nodes))\n\n\tfor _, node := range nodes {\n\t\t// Add node WS path if needed\n\t\tnodeURL := wsURL.String()\n\t\tif !strings.Contains(nodeURL, \"/ws/\") && !strings.HasSuffix(nodeURL, \"/ws\") {\n\t\t\tif strings.HasSuffix(nodeURL, \"/\") {\n\t\t\t\tnodeURL = nodeURL + \"ws\"\n\t\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t\tnodeURL = nodeURL + \"/ws\"\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\n\t\theaders := http.Header{}\n\t\theaders.Set(\"X-Spaxel-Token\", token)\n\n\t\tlog.Printf(\"[SIM] Node %d connecting to %s\", node.ID, nodeURL)\n\n\t\tconn, resp, err := websocket.DefaultDialer.DialContext(ctx, nodeURL, headers)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\tif resp != nil {\n\t\t\t\tbody, _ := io.ReadAll(resp.Body)\n\t\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"node %d dial failed: %w (status %d: %s)\", node.ID, err, resp.StatusCode, string(body))\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"node %d dial failed: %w\", node.ID, err)\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tnode.Conn = conn\n\t\tlog.Printf(\"[SIM] Node %d connected\", node.ID)\n\n\t\t// Send hello message\n\t\thello := map[string]interface{}{\n\t\t\t\"type\":             \"hello\",\n\t\t\t\"mac\":              macToString(node.MAC),\n\t\t\t\"firmware_version\": \"sim-1.0.0\",\n\t\t\t\"capabilities\":     []string{\"csi\", \"tx\", \"rx\"},\n\t\t\t\"chip\":             \"ESP32-S3\",\n\t\t\t\"flash_mb\":         16,\n\t\t\t\"uptime_ms\":        1000,\n\t\t\t\"wifi_rssi\":        -45,\n\t\t\t\"ip\":               fmt.Sprintf(\"127.0.0.%d\", node.ID+2),\n\t\t}\n\n\t\thelloBytes, err := json.Marshal(hello)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\tconn.Close() //nolint:errcheck\n\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"node %d marshal hello: %w\", node.ID, err)\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tnode.mu.Lock()\n\t\terr = conn.WriteMessage(websocket.TextMessage, helloBytes)\n\t\tnode.mu.Unlock()\n\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\tconn.Close() //nolint:errcheck\n\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"node %d send hello: %w\", node.ID, err)\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Wait for role assignment\n\t\tconn.SetReadDeadline(time.Now().Add(10 * time.Second)) //nolint:errcheck\n\t\t_, message, err := conn.ReadMessage()\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\tconn.Close() //nolint:errcheck\n\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"node %d read role: %w\", node.ID, err)\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tvar roleMsg map[string]interface{}\n\t\tif err := json.Unmarshal(message, &roleMsg); err != nil {\n\t\t\tconn.Close() //nolint:errcheck\n\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"node %d parse role: %w\", node.ID, err)\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif roleMsg[\"type\"] == \"reject\" {\n\t\t\tconn.Close() //nolint:errcheck\n\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"node %d rejected: %v\", node.ID, roleMsg[\"reason\"])\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tlog.Printf(\"[SIM] Node %d received role: %v\", node.ID, roleMsg[\"role\"])\n\n\t\t// Start background goroutines for this connection\n\t\tstartTime := time.Now()\n\t\tgo node.pingLoop(ctx)\n\t\tgo node.healthLoop(ctx, startTime)\n\t\tgo node.readLoop(ctx, errChan)\n\t}\n\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// provisionToken provisions a token. Tries the mothership API first,\n// falls back to a synthetic HMAC token.\nfunc provisionToken() (string, error) {\n\t// Parse mothership URL to get HTTP endpoint\n\twsURL, err := url.Parse(*flagMothership)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn \"\", fmt.Errorf(\"invalid mothership URL: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\thttpURL := *wsURL\n\tif httpURL.Scheme == \"ws\" {\n\t\thttpURL.Scheme = \"http\"\n\t} else if httpURL.Scheme == \"wss\" {\n\t\thttpURL.Scheme = \"https\"\n\t}\n\n\t// Trim /ws suffix to get base URL\n\tbaseURL := strings.TrimSuffix(httpURL.String(), \"/ws\")\n\tbaseURL = strings.TrimSuffix(baseURL, \"/\")\n\tprovisionURL := baseURL + \"/api/provision\"\n\n\t// Try POST /api/provision with synthetic credentials\n\tbody := strings.NewReader(`{\"mac\":\"AA:BB:CC:00:00:00\"}`)\n\tresp, err := http.Post(provisionURL, \"application/json\", body)\n\tif err == nil && resp.StatusCode == http.StatusOK {\n\t\tvar result map[string]interface{}\n\t\tif json.NewDecoder(resp.Body).Decode(&result) == nil {\n\t\t\t_ = resp.Body.Close()\n\t\t\tif token, ok := result[\"node_token\"].(string); ok && token != \"\" {\n\t\t\t\treturn token, nil\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\tif resp != nil {\n\t\t_ = resp.Body.Close()\n\t}\n\n\t// Fallback: generate synthetic token\n\th := hmac.New(sha256.New, []byte(\"sim-install-secret\"))\n\th.Write([]byte(\"sim-node\"))\n\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%064x\", h.Sum(nil)), nil\n}\n\n// closeAllNodes closes all node WebSocket connections\nfunc closeAllNodes(nodes []*VirtualNode) {\n\tfor _, node := range nodes {\n\t\tif node.Conn != nil {\n\t\t\tnode.mu.Lock()\n\t\t\tnode.Conn.WriteMessage(websocket.CloseMessage, //nolint:errcheck\n\t\t\t\twebsocket.FormatCloseMessage(websocket.CloseNormalClosure, \"sim shutdown\"))\n\t\t\tnode.Conn.Close() //nolint:errcheck\n\t\t\tnode.mu.Unlock()\n\t\t}\n\t}\n}\n\n// macToString converts a 6-byte MAC to colon-separated hex\nfunc macToString(mac [6]byte) string {\n\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%02X:%02X:%02X:%02X:%02X:%02X\",\n\t\tmac[0], mac[1], mac[2], mac[3], mac[4], mac[5])\n}\n\n// pingLoop sends WebSocket pings\nfunc (n *VirtualNode) pingLoop(ctx context.Context) {\n\tticker := time.NewTicker(30 * time.Second)\n\tdefer ticker.Stop()\n\n\tfor {\n\t\tselect {\n\t\tcase <-ctx.Done():\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\tcase <-ticker.C:\n\t\t\tn.mu.Lock()\n\t\t\terr := n.Conn.WriteMessage(websocket.PingMessage, nil)\n\t\t\tn.mu.Unlock()\n\n\t\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[SIM] Node %d ping failed: %v\", n.ID, err)\n\t\t\t\treturn\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n}\n\n// healthLoop sends periodic health messages\nfunc (n *VirtualNode) healthLoop(ctx context.Context, startTime time.Time) {\n\tticker := time.NewTicker(10 * time.Second)\n\tdefer ticker.Stop()\n\n\tfor {\n\t\tselect {\n\t\tcase <-ctx.Done():\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\tcase <-ticker.C:\n\t\t\thealth := map[string]interface{}{\n\t\t\t\t\"type\":            \"health\",\n\t\t\t\t\"mac\":             macToString(n.MAC),\n\t\t\t\t\"timestamp_ms\":    time.Now().UnixMilli(),\n\t\t\t\t\"free_heap_bytes\": 200000,\n\t\t\t\t\"wifi_rssi_dbm\":   -45,\n\t\t\t\t\"uptime_ms\":       time.Since(startTime).Milliseconds(),\n\t\t\t\t\"csi_rate_hz\":     *flagRate,\n\t\t\t\t\"wifi_channel\":    *flagChannel,\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\thealthBytes, err := json.Marshal(health)\n\t\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[SIM] Node %d marshal health: %v\", n.ID, err)\n\t\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\tn.mu.Lock()\n\t\t\terr = n.Conn.WriteMessage(websocket.TextMessage, healthBytes)\n\t\t\tn.mu.Unlock()\n\n\t\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[SIM] Node %d send health failed: %v\", n.ID, err)\n\t\t\t\treturn\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n}\n\n// readLoop reads downstream messages from the WebSocket\nfunc (n *VirtualNode) readLoop(ctx context.Context, errChan chan<- error) {\n\tfor {\n\t\tselect {\n\t\tcase <-ctx.Done():\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\tdefault:\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tn.mu.Lock()\n\t\tconn := n.Conn\n\t\tn.mu.Unlock()\n\n\t\tif conn == nil {\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tconn.SetReadDeadline(time.Now().Add(60 * time.Second)) //nolint:errcheck\n\t\t_, message, err := conn.ReadMessage()\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\tselect {\n\t\t\tcase <-ctx.Done():\n\t\t\t\treturn\n\t\t\tdefault:\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tif websocket.IsCloseError(err) {\n\t\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[SIM] Node %d connection closed\", n.ID)\n\t\t\t\treturn\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[SIM] Node %d read error: %v\", n.ID, err)\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tvar msg map[string]interface{}\n\t\tif err := json.Unmarshal(message, &msg); err != nil {\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tmsgType, ok := msg[\"type\"].(string)\n\t\tif !ok {\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tswitch msgType {\n\t\tcase \"role\":\n\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[SIM] Node %d role update: %v\", n.ID, msg[\"role\"])\n\t\tcase \"config\":\n\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[SIM] Node %d config update: %v\", n.ID, msg)\n\t\tcase \"reject\":\n\t\t\terrChan <- fmt.Errorf(\"node %d rejected: %v\", n.ID, msg[\"reason\"])\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\tcase \"shutdown\":\n\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[SIM] Node %d received shutdown\", n.ID)\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\t}\n}\n\n// runSimulation runs the main CSI generation loop\nfunc runSimulation(ctx context.Context, nodes []*VirtualNode, walkers []*Walker, space *Space, rng *rand.Rand, csvWriter *CSVWriter, stats *Stats, done chan<- struct{}, scenario *ScenarioConfig) {\n\tdefer close(done)\n\n\tticker := time.NewTicker(time.Duration(1000/(*flagRate)) * time.Millisecond)\n\tdefer ticker.Stop()\n\n\tframeNum := 0\n\tlastBLETime := time.Now()\n\n\t// Initialize fall scenario state\n\tvar fallState *FallScenarioState\n\tif scenario.Type == ScenarioFall || scenario.Type == ScenarioBagOnCouch {\n\t\tif len(walkers) > 0 {\n\t\t\tfallState = &FallScenarioState{\n\t\t\t\tWalker: walkers[0],\n\t\t\t\tState:  \"walking\",\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tif scenario.Type == ScenarioBagOnCouch {\n\t\t\t\t// For bag-on-couch, start with a lower position\n\t\t\t\twalkers[0].Position.Z = 1.0\n\t\t\t\twalkers[0].Velocity.Z = -0.2 // Slow descent\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Track scenario timing\n\tscenarioStarted := false\n\n\tfor {\n\t\tselect {\n\t\tcase <-ctx.Done():\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\tcase <-ticker.C:\n\t\t\t// Handle fall scenario timing\n\t\t\tif fallState != nil && !scenarioStarted && time.Since(scenario.StartedAt) >= scenario.FallParams.TriggerAfter {\n\t\t\t\tif scenario.Type == ScenarioFall {\n\t\t\t\t\tfallState.StartFall(scenario.FallParams)\n\t\t\t\t\tscenarioStarted = true\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t// Update walker positions\n\t\t\tif fallState != nil {\n\t\t\t\tdt := 1.0 / float64(*flagRate)\n\t\t\t\tfallState.UpdateForFallScenario(dt, scenario.FallParams, space, rng)\n\t\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t\tupdateWalkers(walkers, space, rng)\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t// Write to CSV\n\t\t\tif csvWriter != nil {\n\t\t\t\tcsvWriter.WriteRow(walkers, nodes, walls)\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t// Send CSI frames for each node pair\n\t\t\tfor _, txNode := range nodes {\n\t\t\t\tfor _, rxNode := range nodes {\n\t\t\t\t\tif txNode.ID == rxNode.ID {\n\t\t\t\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t\t\tframe := generateCSIFrame(txNode, rxNode, walkers, walls, frameNum, rng)\n\n\t\t\t\t\ttxNode.mu.Lock()\n\t\t\t\t\terr := txNode.Conn.WriteMessage(websocket.BinaryMessage, frame)\n\t\t\t\t\ttxNode.mu.Unlock()\n\n\t\t\t\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\t\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[SIM] Node %d send CSI failed: %v\", txNode.ID, err)\n\t\t\t\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t\t\tstats.FramesSent.Add(1)\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t// Send BLE messages if enabled\n\t\t\tif *flagBLE && time.Since(lastBLETime) > 5*time.Second {\n\t\t\t\tsendBLEMessages(nodes, walkers)\n\t\t\t\tlastBLETime = time.Now()\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\tframeNum++\n\t\t}\n\t}\n}\n\n// updateWalkers updates walker positions based on their movement type\nfunc updateWalkers(walkers []*Walker, space *Space, rng *rand.Rand) {\n\tdt := 1.0 / float64(*flagRate)\n\n\tfor _, walker := range walkers {\n\t\tswitch walker.Type {\n\t\tcase WalkerTypePathFollow:\n\t\t\tupdatePathWalker(walker, dt)\n\t\tcase WalkerTypeNodeToNode:\n\t\t\tupdateNodeToNodeWalker(walker, dt, space)\n\t\tcase WalkerTypeRandomWalk:\n\t\t\tupdateRandomWalker(walker, dt, space, rng)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n}\n\n// updateRandomWalker implements random walk motion\nfunc updateRandomWalker(walker *Walker, dt float64, space *Space, rng *rand.Rand) {\n\twalker.Position.X += walker.Velocity.X * dt\n\twalker.Position.Y += walker.Velocity.Y * dt\n\n\t// Bounce off walls\n\tmargin := 0.2\n\tif walker.Position.X < margin {\n\t\twalker.Position.X = margin\n\t\twalker.Velocity.X *= -1\n\t}\n\tif walker.Position.X > space.Width-margin {\n\t\twalker.Position.X = space.Width - margin\n\t\twalker.Velocity.X *= -1\n\t}\n\tif walker.Position.Y < margin {\n\t\twalker.Position.Y = margin\n\t\twalker.Velocity.Y *= -1\n\t}\n\tif walker.Position.Y > space.Depth-margin {\n\t\twalker.Position.Y = space.Depth - margin\n\t\twalker.Velocity.Y *= -1\n\t}\n\n\t// Random velocity perturbation\n\tperturbation := 0.1\n\twalker.Velocity.X += (rng.Float64() - 0.5) * perturbation\n\twalker.Velocity.Y += (rng.Float64() - 0.5) * perturbation\n\n\t// Clamp velocity\n\tspeed := walker.Speed * (0.5 + rng.Float64()*0.5)\n\tcurrentSpeed := math.Sqrt(walker.Velocity.X*walker.Velocity.X + walker.Velocity.Y*walker.Velocity.Y)\n\tif currentSpeed > 0 {\n\t\twalker.Velocity.X = (walker.Velocity.X / currentSpeed) * speed\n\t\twalker.Velocity.Y = (walker.Velocity.Y / currentSpeed) * speed\n\t}\n\n\twalker.Position.Z = walker.Height\n}\n\n// updatePathWalker implements path-following motion\nfunc updatePathWalker(walker *Walker, dt float64) {\n\tif len(walker.Path) == 0 {\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\ttarget := walker.Path[walker.PathIdx]\n\tdx := target.X - walker.Position.X\n\tdy := target.Y - walker.Position.Y\n\tdz := target.Z - walker.Position.Z\n\tdist := math.Sqrt(dx*dx + dy*dy + dz*dz)\n\n\t// If very close to target, move to next waypoint\n\tif dist < 0.1 {\n\t\twalker.PathIdx = (walker.PathIdx + 1) % len(walker.Path)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Move towards target at constant speed\n\tmoveDist := walker.Speed * dt\n\tif moveDist > dist {\n\t\tmoveDist = dist\n\t}\n\n\tt := moveDist / dist\n\twalker.Position.X += dx * t\n\twalker.Position.Y += dy * t\n\n\t// Update velocity vector for consistency\n\tif dist > 0 {\n\t\twalker.Velocity.X = (dx / dist) * walker.Speed\n\t\twalker.Velocity.Y = (dy / dist) * walker.Speed\n\t\twalker.Velocity.Z = (dz / dist) * walker.Speed\n\t}\n}\n\n// updateNodeToNodeWalker implements traversal between virtual nodes\nfunc updateNodeToNodeWalker(walker *Walker, dt float64, space *Space) {\n\tif len(walker.Nodes) < 2 {\n\t\tupdateRandomWalker(walker, dt, space, rand.New(rand.NewSource(time.Now().UnixNano())))\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\ttargetNode := walker.Nodes[walker.NodeIdx]\n\ttargetPos := targetNode.Position\n\n\tdx := targetPos.X - walker.Position.X\n\tdy := targetPos.Y - walker.Position.Y\n\tdz := targetPos.Z - walker.Position.Z\n\tdist := math.Sqrt(dx*dx + dy*dy + dz*dz)\n\n\t// Check if we've arrived at the target node (horizontal distance)\n\thorizontalDist := math.Sqrt(dx*dx + dy*dy)\n\tif horizontalDist < 0.3 {\n\t\t// Move to next node\n\t\twalker.NodeIdx = (walker.NodeIdx + 1) % len(walker.Nodes)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Move towards target node\n\tmoveDist := walker.Speed * dt\n\tif moveDist > horizontalDist {\n\t\tmoveDist = horizontalDist\n\t}\n\n\tt := moveDist / horizontalDist\n\twalker.Position.X += dx * t\n\twalker.Position.Y += dy * t\n\n\t// Update velocity vector\n\tif horizontalDist > 0 {\n\t\twalker.Velocity.X = (dx / horizontalDist) * walker.Speed\n\t\twalker.Velocity.Y = (dy / horizontalDist) * walker.Speed\n\t\twalker.Velocity.Z = (dz / dist) * walker.Speed\n\t}\n\n\twalker.Position.Z = walker.Height\n}\n\n// sendBLEMessages sends synthetic BLE scan results\nfunc sendBLEMessages(nodes []*VirtualNode, walkers []*Walker) {\n\tfor _, node := range nodes {\n\t\tdevices := make([]map[string]interface{}, 0)\n\n\t\tfor _, walker := range walkers {\n\t\t\tdx := walker.Position.X - node.Position.X\n\t\t\tdy := walker.Position.Y - node.Position.Y\n\t\t\tdz := walker.Position.Z - node.Position.Z\n\t\t\tdist := math.Sqrt(dx*dx + dy*dy + dz*dz)\n\n\t\t\trssi := -50.0 - 20.0*math.Log10(dist/1.0)\n\t\t\tif rssi < -90 {\n\t\t\t\trssi = -90\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\tdevices = append(devices, map[string]interface{}{\n\t\t\t\t\"addr\": fmt.Sprintf(\"AA:BB:CC:DD:EE:%02X\", walker.ID),\n\t\t\t\t\"rssi\": int(rssi),\n\t\t\t\t\"name\": fmt.Sprintf(\"sim-person-%d\", walker.ID),\n\t\t\t})\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif len(devices) == 0 {\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tbleMsg := map[string]interface{}{\n\t\t\t\"type\":         \"ble\",\n\t\t\t\"mac\":          macToString(node.MAC),\n\t\t\t\"timestamp_ms\": time.Now().UnixMilli(),\n\t\t\t\"devices\":      devices,\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tbleBytes, err := json.Marshal(bleMsg)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[SIM] Node %d marshal BLE: %v\", node.ID, err)\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tnode.mu.Lock()\n\t\terr = node.Conn.WriteMessage(websocket.TextMessage, bleBytes)\n\t\tnode.mu.Unlock()\n\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[SIM] Node %d send BLE failed: %v\", node.ID, err)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n}\n\n// reportStats periodically prints statistics\nfunc reportStats(ctx context.Context, stats *Stats) {\n\tticker := time.NewTicker(10 * time.Second)\n\tdefer ticker.Stop()\n\n\tfor {\n\t\tselect {\n\t\tcase <-ctx.Done():\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\tcase <-ticker.C:\n\t\t\tnow := time.Now()\n\t\t\telapsed := now.Sub(stats.StartTime).Seconds()\n\t\t\tframesSent := stats.FramesSent.Load()\n\n\t\t\tif elapsed > 0 {\n\t\t\t\tfps := float64(framesSent) / elapsed\n\t\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[SIM] Stats: frames=%d fps=%.1f elapsed=%.1fs\", framesSent, fps, elapsed)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n}\n\n// printFinalStats prints final simulation statistics\nfunc printFinalStats(stats *Stats, walkerCount int) {\n\telapsed := time.Since(stats.StartTime).Seconds()\n\tframesSent := stats.FramesSent.Load()\n\n\tlog.Printf(\"[SIM] Final Statistics:\")\n\tlog.Printf(\"[SIM]   Frames sent: %d\", framesSent)\n\tlog.Printf(\"[SIM]   Duration: %.1f seconds\", elapsed)\n\tif elapsed > 0 {\n\t\tlog.Printf(\"[SIM]   Average FPS: %.1f\", float64(framesSent)/elapsed)\n\t}\n\tlog.Printf(\"[SIM]   Walkers: %d\", walkerCount)\n}\n\nfunc min(a, b int) int {\n\tif a < b {\n\t\treturn a\n\t}\n\treturn b\n}\n\n// toSimulatorPoint converts a main.Point to simulator.Point\nfunc toSimulatorPoint(p Point) simulator.Point {\n\treturn simulator.Point{X: p.X, Y: p.Y, Z: p.Z}\n}\n\n// toSimulatorSpace converts a main.Space to simulator.Space\nfunc toSimulatorSpace(s *Space) *simulator.Space {\n\treturn &simulator.Space{\n\t\tID:   \"sim-space\",\n\t\tName: \"Simulation Space\",\n\t\tRooms: []simulator.Room{{\n\t\t\tID:   \"room-1\",\n\t\t\tName: \"Main Room\",\n\t\t\tMinX: 0, MinY: 0, MinZ: 0,\n\t\t\tMaxX: s.Width, MaxY: s.Depth, MaxZ: s.Height,\n\t\t}},\n\t}\n}\n\n// outputGDOPOverlay computes and outputs GDOP overlay data as JSON\nfunc outputGDOPOverlay(space *Space, nodes []*VirtualNode, cellSize float64) error {\n\t// Convert to simulator types\n\tsimSpace := toSimulatorSpace(space)\n\tnodeSet := simulator.NewNodeSet()\n\tfor _, vn := range nodes {\n\t\tsimPos := toSimulatorPoint(vn.Position)\n\t\tnodeID := fmt.Sprintf(\"node-%d\", vn.ID)\n\t\tnodeName := fmt.Sprintf(\"Node %d\", vn.ID)\n\t\tnodeSet.AddVirtualNode(nodeID, nodeName, simPos)\n\t}\n\n\t// Generate links\n\tlinks := simulator.GenerateAllLinks(nodeSet)\n\n\tif len(links) < 2 {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"need at least 2 nodes for GDOP computation\")\n\t}\n\n\t// Get space bounds from simulator space\n\tminX, minY, _, maxX, maxY, _ := simSpace.Bounds()\n\n\t// Create GDOP computer\n\tconfig := simulator.GridConfig{\n\t\tMinX:     minX,\n\t\tMinY:     minY,\n\t\tWidth:    maxX - minX,\n\t\tDepth:    maxY - minY,\n\t\tCellSize: cellSize,\n\t}\n\tgdopComp := simulator.NewGDOPComputer(links, config)\n\n\t// Compute GDOP for all cells\n\tresults := gdopComp.ComputeAll()\n\n\t// Convert to heatmap data\n\theatmapData := gdopComp.ToHeatmapData(results)\n\n\t// Get average GDOP, handling infinity\n\tavgGDOP := gdopComp.AverageGDOP(results)\n\tavgGDOPOutput := \"Infinity\"\n\tif !math.IsInf(avgGDOP, 0) {\n\t\tavgGDOPOutput = fmt.Sprintf(\"%.2f\", avgGDOP)\n\t}\n\n\t// Output JSON\n\toutput := map[string]interface{}{\n\t\t\"type\":            \"gdop_overlay\",\n\t\t\"space_dimensions\": map[string]float64{\n\t\t\t\"width_m\":  space.Width,\n\t\t\t\"depth_m\":  space.Depth,\n\t\t\t\"height_m\": space.Height,\n\t\t},\n\t\t\"grid_dimensions\": []int{heatmapData.Width, heatmapData.Depth, 1},\n\t\t\"cell_size_m\":    cellSize,\n\t\t\"origin\":         map[string]float64{\"x\": heatmapData.OriginX, \"y\": heatmapData.OriginY},\n\t\t\"gdop_values\":    heatmapData.GDOPValues,\n\t\t\"qualities\":      heatmapData.Qualities,\n\t\t\"colors\":         heatmapData.Colors,\n\t\t\"accuracy_map\":   heatmapData.AccuracyMap,\n\t\t\"coverage_score\": gdopComp.CoverageScore(results),\n\t\t\"average_gdop\":   avgGDOPOutput,\n\t\t\"quality_counts\": gdopComp.QualityCounts(results),\n\t\t\"dead_zones\":     gdopComp.FindDeadZones(results),\n\t\t\"links\":          links,\n\t\t\"timestamp\":      time.Now().Format(time.RFC3339),\n\t}\n\n\tenc := json.NewEncoder(os.Stdout)\n\tenc.SetIndent(\"\", \"  \")\n\treturn enc.Encode(output)\n}\n\n// outputShoppingList generates and outputs the shopping list as JSON\nfunc outputShoppingList(space *Space, nodes []*VirtualNode) error {\n\t// Convert to simulator types\n\tsimSpace := toSimulatorSpace(space)\n\tnodeSet := simulator.NewNodeSet()\n\tfor _, vn := range nodes {\n\t\tsimPos := shoppingList,\n\t\t\"timestamp\": time.Now().Format(time.RFC3339),\n\t\t\"note\":     \"This is a pre-deployment estimate. For passive links (AP as TX), the AP is always first.\nfunc (l Link) CanonicalID() string {\n\tif l.TX.IsAP() {\n\t\t// AP is always first component\n\t\treturn l.TX.ID + \":\" + l.RX.ID\n\t}\n\t// Sort lexicographically for bidirectional links\n\tif l.TX.ID < l.RX.ID {\n\t\treturn l.TX.ID + \":\" + l.RX.ID\n\t}\n\treturn l.RX.ID + \":\" + l.TX.ID\n}\n\n// IsBidirectional returns true if this link represents bidirectional communication\n// (both nodes can TX and RX)\nfunc (l Link) IsBidirectional() bool {\n\treturn (l.TX.Role == RoleTXRX || l.TX.Role == RoleTX) &&\n\t\t(l.RX.Role == RoleTXRX || l.RX.Role == RoleRX)\n}\n\n// IsPassive returns true if this is a passive radar link (AP as TX)\nfunc (l Link) IsPassive() bool {\n\treturn l.TX.IsAP() && (l.RX.Role == RoleRX || l.RX.Role == RoleTXRX || l.RX.Role == RolePassive)\n}\n","numLines":47,"startLine":1,"totalLines":47}}}

float64 // Grid cell size in meters\n20\t\tMinX, MinY  float64 // Grid origin\n21\t\tWidth       float64 // Grid width\n22\t\tDepth       float64 // Grid depth\n23\t}\n24\t\n25\t// GDOPComputer consider (default 3)\n30\t}\n31\t\n32\t// NewGDOPComputer creates a new GDOP computer\n33\tfunc NewGDOPComputer(links []Link, config GridConfig) *GDOPComputer {\n34\t\tif config.CellSize <= 0 {\n35\t\t\tconfig.CellSize = 0.2 // Default 20cm\n36\t\t}\n37\t\treturn &GDOPComputer{\n38\t\t\tlinks:   links,\n39\t\t\tconfig:  config,\n40\t\t\tmaxZone: 3, // Default: consider first 3 Fresnel zones\n41\t\t}\n42\t}\n43\t\n44\t// SetMaxZone sets the maximum Fresnel zone to consider\n45\tfunc (gc *GDOPComputer) SetMaxZone(zone int) {\n46\t\tif zone < 1 {\n47\t\t\tzone = 1\n48\t\t}\n49\t\tgc.maxZone = zone\n50\t}\n51\t\n52\t// ComputeAll computes GDOP for the entire grid\n53\t// Returns a slice of GDOP results indexed by cell position\n54\tfunc (gc *GDOPComputer) ComputeAll() [][]GDOPResult {\n55\t\tnx := int(math.Ceil(gc.config.Width / gc.config.CellSize))\n56\t\tny := int(math.Ceil(gc.config.Depth / gc.config.CellSize))\n57\t\n58\t\tresults := make([][]GDOPResult, ny)\n59\t\n60\t\tfor iy := 0; iy < ny; iy++ {\n61\t\t\tresults[iy] = make([]GDOPResult, nx)\n62\t\t\tfor ix := 0; ix < nx; ix++ {\n63\t\t\t\tx := gc.config.MinX + (float64(ix)+0.5)*gc.config.CellSize\n64\t\t\t\ty := gc.config.MinY + (float64(iy)+0.5)*gc.config.CellSize\n65\t\t\t\tz := 1.0 // Use 1m height for 2D GDOP analysis\n66\t\n67\t\t\t\tresult := gc.ComputeAt(x, y, z)\n68\t\t\t\tresults[iy][ix] = result\n69\t\t\t}\n70\t\t}\n71\t\n72\t\treturn results\n73\t}\n74\t\n75\t// ComputeAt computes GDOP at a specific point\n76\tfunc (gc *GDOPComputer) ComputeAt(x, y, z float64) GDOPResult {\n77\t\tpoint := Point{X: x, Y: y, Z: z}\n78\t\n79\t\t// Collect links that cover this point (within maxZone Fresnel zones)\n80\t\tvar coveringLinks []Link\n81\t\tvar linkIDs []string\n82\t\n83\t\tfor _, link := range gc.links {\n84\t\t\tif IsInFresnelZones(link.TX.Position, link.RX.Position, point, gc.maxZone) {\n85\t\t\t\tcoveringLinks = append(coveringLinks, link)\n86\t\t\t\tlinkIDs = append(linkIDs, link.TX.ID+\":\"+link.RX.ID)\n87\t\t\t}\n88\t\t}\n89\t\n90\t\tresult := GDOPResult{\n91\t\t\tX:                 x,\n92\t\t\tY:                 y,\n93\t\t\tZ:                 z,\n94\t\t\tContributingLinks: linkIDs,\n95\t\t}\n96\t\n97\t\tif len(coveringLinks) < 2 {\n98\t\t\t// Need at least 2 links for 2D localization\n99\t\t\tresult.GDOP = math.Inf(1)\n100\t\t\tresult.Quality = \"none\"\n101\t\t\treturn result\n102\t\t}\n103\t\n104\t\t// Compute GDOP using angular diversity\n105\t\tgdop := gc.computeGDOPAngular(point, coveringLinks)\n106\t\tresult.GDOP = gdop\n107\t\tresult.Quality = gdopToQuality(gdop)\n108\t\n109\t\treturn result\n110\t}\n111\t\n112\t// computeGDOPAngular computes GDOP based on angular diversity of link directions\n113\t// This is the 2D GDOP formula from the plan\n114\tfunc (gc *GDOPComputer) computeGDOPAngular(point Point, links []Link) float64 {\n115\t\t// Step 1: Collect link angles\n116\t\t// For each link, compute the angle of the line from TX to RX as seen from point\n117\t\ttype linkAngle struct {\n118\t\t\ttheta float64 // angle in radians\n119\t\t\tlink  Link\n120\t\t}\n121\t\n122\t\tangles := make([]linkAngle, 0, len(links))\n123\t\tfor _, link := range links {\n124\t\t\t// Project to floor plane (ignore Z for 2D analysis)\n125\t\t\tdx := link.RX.Position.X - link.TX.Position.X\n126\t\t\tdy := link.RX.Position.Y - link.TX.Position.Y\n127\t\t\ttheta := math.Atan2(dy, dx)\n128\t\t\tangles = append(angles, linkAngle{theta: theta, link: link})\n129\t\t}\n130\t\n131\t\tif len(angles) < 2 {\n132\t\t\treturn math.Inf(1)\n133\t\t}\n134\t\n135\t\t// Step 2: Build Fisher information matrix\n136\t\t// F = Σ [ [cos²(θ),       cos(θ)·sin(θ)],\n137\t\t//        [cos(θ)·sin(θ), sin²(θ)       ] ]\n138\t\tvar f00, f01, f11 float64\n139\t\n140\t\tfor _, la := range angles {\n141\t\t\tc := math.Cos(la.theta)\n142\t\t\ts := math.Sin(la.theta)\n143\t\n144\t\t\tf00 += c * c\n145\t\t\tf01 += c * s\n146\t\t\tf11 += s * s\n147\t\t}\n148\t\n149\t\t// Step 3: Compute determinant\n150\t\tdet := f00*f11 - f01*f01\n151\t\n152\t\t// Check for degenerate geometry (collinear links)\n153\t\tif det <= 1e-6 {\n154\t\t\treturn math.Inf(1)\n155\t\t}\n156\t\n157\t\t// Step 4: Compute trace of F^-1\n158\t\t// For 2x2 matrix: trace(F^-1) = (f00 + f11) / det\n159\t\ttraceFInv := (f00 + f11) / det\n160\t\n161\t\t// Step 5: GDOP = sqrt(trace(F^-1))\n162\t\tgdop := math.Sqrt(traceFInv)\n163\t\n164\t\treturn gdop\n165\t}\n166\t\n167\t// gdopToQuality converts GDOP value to quality string\n168\tfunc gdopToQuality(gdop float64) string {\n169\t\tif math.IsInf(gdop, 0) {\n170\t\t\treturn \"none\"\n171\t\t}\n172\t\tif gdop < 2.0 {\n173\t\t\treturn \"excellent\"\n174\t\t}\n175\t\tif gdop < 4.0 {\n176\t\t\treturn \"good\"\n177\t\t}\n178\t\tif gdop < 8.0 {\n179\t\t\treturn \"fair\"\n180\t\t}\n181\t\treturn \"poor\"\n182\t}\n183\t\n184\t// CoverageScore computes the percentage of cells with \"good\" or better coverage\n185\tfunc (gc *GDOPComputer) CoverageScore(results [][]GDOPResult) float64 {\n186\t\tif len(results) == 0 || len(results[0]) == 0 {\n187\t\t\treturn 0\n188\t\t}\n189\t\n190\t\tgoodCells := 0\n191\t\ttotalCells := 0\n192\t\n193\t\tfor _, row := range results {\n194\t\t\tfor _, cell := range row {\n195\t\t\t\ttotalCells++\n196\t\t\t\tif cell.Quality == \"excellent\" || cell.Quality == \"good\" {\n197\t\t\t\t\tgoodCells++\n198\t\t\t\t}\n199\t\t\t}\n200\t\t}\n201\t\n202\t\treturn 100.0 * float64(goodCells) / float64(totalCells)\n203\t}\n204\t\n205\t// AverageGDOP computes the average GDOP over all cells (excluding infinity)\n206\tfunc (gc *GDOPComputer) AverageGDOP(results [][]GDOPResult) float64 {\n207\t\tsum := 0.0\n208\t\tcount := 0\n209\t\n210\t\tfor _, row := range results {\n211\t\t\tfor _, cell := range row {\n212\t\t\t\tif !math.IsInf(cell.GDOP, 0) {\n213\t\t\t\t\tsum += cell.GDOP\n214\t\t\t\t\tcount++\n215\t\t\t\t}\n216\t\t\t}\n217\t\t}\n218\t\n219\t\tif count == 0 {\n220\t\t\treturn math.Inf(1)\n221\t\t}\n222\t\treturn sum / float64(count)\n223\t}\n224\t\n225\t// QualityCounts returns the count of cells by quality level\n226\tfunc (gc *GDOPComputer) QualityCounts(results [][]GDOPResult) map[string]int {\n227\t\tcounts := map[string]int{\n228\t\t\t\"excellent\": 0,\n229\t\t\t\"good\":      0,\n230\t\t\t\"fair\":      0,\n231\t\t\t\"poor\":      0,\n232\t\t\t\"none\":      0,\n233\t\t}\n234\t\n235\t\tfor _, row := range results {\n236\t\t\tfor _, cell := range row {\n237\t\t\t\tcounts[cell.Quality]++\n238\t\t\t}\n239\t\t}\n240\t\n241\t\treturn counts\n242\t}\n243\t\n244\t// FindDeadZones returns positions where coverage is \"none\" or \"poor\"\n245\tfunc (gc *GDOPComputer) FindDeadZones(results [][]GDOPResult) []Point {\n246\t\tdeadZones := make([]Point, 0)\n247\t\n248\t\tfor _, row := range results {\n249\t\t\tfor _, cell := range row {\n250\t\t\t\tif cell.Quality == \"none\" || cell.Quality == \"poor\" {\n251\t\t\t\t\tdeadZones = append(deadZones, Point{X: cell.X, Y: cell.Y, Z: cell.Z})\n252\t\t\t\t}\n253\t\t\t}\n254\t\t}\n255\t\n256\t\treturn deadZones\n257\t}\n258\t\n259\t// RecommendNodePosition suggests optimal positions for additional nodes\n260\t// based on current dead zones\n261\tfunc (gc *GDOPComputer) RecommendNodePosition(results [][]GDOPResult, space *Space) Point {\n262\t\t// Find the centroid of the largest dead zone\n263\t\tdeadZones := gc.FindDeadZones(results)\n264\t\tif len(deadZones) == 0 {\n265\t\t\t// No dead zones, suggest center of space\n266\t\t\tminX, minY, _, maxX, maxY, _ := space.Bounds()\n267\t\t\treturn Point{\n268\t\t\t\tX: (minX + maxX) / 2,\n269\t\t\t\tY: (minY + maxY) / 2,\n270\t\t\t\tZ: 2.0, // Suggest high placement\n271\t\t\t}\n272\t\t}\n273\t\n274\t\t// Cluster dead zones and find the largest cluster\n275\t\t// Simplified: just return the centroid of all dead zones\n276\t\tvar sumX, sumY float64\n277\t\tfor _, dz := range deadZones {\n278\t\t\tsumX += dz.X\n279\t\t\tsumY += dz.Y\n280\t\t}\n281\t\n282\t\tcentroid := Point{\n283\t\t\tX: sumX / float64(len(deadZones)),\n284\t\t\tY: sumY / float64(len(deadZones)),\n285\t\t\tZ: 2.0, // Suggest high placement for better coverage\n286\t\t}\n287\t\n288\t\treturn centroid\n289\t}\n290\t\n291\t// MinimumNodeCount estimates the minimum number of nodes needed for good coverage\n292\t// Based on space dimensions and desired quality threshold\n293\tfunc MinimumNodeCount(space *Space, targetGDOP float64) int {\n294\t\twidth, depth, _ := space.Dimensions()\n295\t\tarea := width * depth\n296\t\n297\t\t// Heuristic: nodes needed based on area and desired GDOP\n298\t\t// For GDOP < 4 (good coverage): approximately 1 node per 15-20 m²\n299\t\tif targetGDOP < 2 {\n300\t\t\t// Excellent coverage requires more nodes\n301\t\t\treturn int(math.Ceil(area / 15.0))\n302\t\t}\n303\t\tif targetGDOP < 4 {\n304\t\t\t// Good coverage\n305\t\t\treturn int(math.Ceil(area / 20.0))\n306\t\t}\n307\t\t// Fair coverage\n308\t\treturn int(math.Ceil(area / 30.0))\n309\t}\n310\t\n311\t// ExpectedAccuracy estimates the expected localization accuracy at a point\n312\t// based on its GDOP value\n313\tfunc ExpectedAccuracy(gdop float64) float64 {\n314\t\tif math.IsInf(gdop, 0) {\n315\t\t\treturn math.Inf(1)\n316\t\t}\n317\t\n318\t\t// Based on research: typical CSI accuracy with 4+ nodes is ±0.5-1.0m\n319\t\t// GDOP < 2: ±0.5m, GDOP 2-4: ±1.0m, GDOP > 4: degrades further\n320\t\tbaseAccuracy := 0.5 // meters for GDOP = 1\n321\t\n322\t\treturn baseAccuracy * gdop\n323\t}\n324\t\n325\t// GDOPColor represents a color for GDOP visualization\n326\ttype GDOPColor struct {\n327\t\tR, G, B uint8 // RGB values 0-255\n328\t}\n329\t\n330\t// GDOPColorMap returns the color for a given GDOP value for visualization\n331\t// Uses: green (excellent), yellow (good), orange (fair), red (poor), gray (none)\n332\tfunc GDOPColorMap(gdop float64) GDOPColor {\n333\t\tif math.IsInf(gdop, 0) {\n334\t\t\treturn GDOPColor{R: 80, G: 80, B: 80} // Gray for no coverage\n335\t\t}\n336\t\tif gdop < 2.0 {\n337\t\t\treturn GDOPColor{R: 34, G: 197, B: 94} // Green (#22c65e) for excellent\n338\t\t}\n339\t\tif gdop < 4.0 {\n340\t\t\treturn GDOPColor{R: 255, G: 193, B: 7} // Yellow (#ffc107) for format\n362\tfunc (gc *GDOPComputer) ToHeatmapData(results [][]GDOPResult) *GDOPHeatmapData {\n363\t\tif len(results) == 0 || len(results[0]) == 0 {\n364\t\t\treturn &GDOPHeatmapData{}\n365\t\t}\n366\t\n367\t\tdepth := len(results)    // rows (Y)\n368\t\twidth := len(results[0]) // cols (X)\n369\t\ttotalCells := width * depth\n370\t\n371\t\tdata := &GDOPHeatmapData{\n372\t\t\tWidth:       width,\n373\t\t\tDepth:       depth,\n374\t\t\tCellSize:    gc.config.CellSize,\n375\t\t\tOriginX:     gc.config.MinX,\n376\t\t\tOriginY:     gc.config.MinY,\n377\t\t\tGDOPValues:  make([]float64, totalCells),\n378\t\t\tQualities:   make([]string, totalCells),\n379\t\t\tColors:      make([][]uint8, totalCells),\n380\t\t\tAccuracyMap: make([]float64, totalCells),\n381\t\t}\n382\t\n383\t\tfor y := 0; y < depth; y++ {\n384\t\t\tfor x := 0; x < width; x++ {\n385\t\t\t\tidx := y*width + x\n386\t\t\t\tresult := results[y][x]\n387\t\n388\t\t\t\t// GDOP value (9999 for infinity)\n389\t\t\t\tif math.IsInf(result.GDOP, 0) {\n390\t\t\t\t\tdata.GDOPValues[idx] = 9999.0\n391\t\t\t\t} else {\n392\t\t\t\t\tdata.GDOPValues[idx] = result.GDOP\n393\t\t\t\t}\n394\t\n395\t\t\t\t// Quality string\n396\t\t\t\tdata.Qualities[idx] = result.Quality\n397\t\n398\t\t\t\t// RGB color\n399\t\t\t\tcolor ExpectedAccuracy(result.GDOP)\n404\t\t\t\tif math.IsInf(accuracy, 0) {\n405\t\t\t\t\tdata.AccuracyMap[idx] = 9999.0\n406\t\t\t\t} else {\n407\t\t\t\t\tdata.AccuracyMap[idx] = values in meters (infinity = no coverage)\n417\tfunc (gc *GDOPComputer) ComputeAccuracyMap(results [][]GDOPResult) [][]float64 {\n418\t\tif len(results) == 0 {\n419\t\t\treturn nil\n420\t\t}\n421\t\n422\t\taccuracyMap := make([][]float64, len(results))\n423\t\tfor i := range results {\n424\t\t\taccuracyMap[i] = make([]float64, len(results[i]))\n425\t\t\tfor j := range the N cells with the worst GDOP values\n458\tfunc (gc *GDOPComputer) GetWorstCoverageCells(results [][]GDOPResult, n int) []GDOPResult {\n459\t\tif len(results) == 0 {\n460\t\t\treturn nil\n461\t\t}\n462\t\n463\t\t// Flatten all cells\n464\t\tcells := make([]GDOPResult, 0)\n465\t\tfor _, row := range results {\n466\t\t\tcells = append(cells, row...)\n467\t\t}\n468\t\n469\t\t// Sort by GDOP (descending, so worst first)\n470\t\tfor i := 0; i < len(cells); i++ {\n471\t\t\tfor j := i + 1; j < len(cells); j++ {\n472\t\t\t\t// Handle infinity: infinity is worse than any finite value\n473\t\t\t\tiInf := math.IsInf(cells[i].GDOP, 0)\n474\t\t\t\tjInf := math.IsInf(cells[j].GDOP, 0)\n475\t\n476\t\t\t\tvar swap bool\n477\t\t\t\tif iInf && !jInf {\n478\t\t\t\t\tswap = false // i stays (infinity at top)\n479\t\t\t\t} else if !iInf && jInf {\n480\t\t\t\t\tswap = true // j is infinity, should be before i\n481\t\t\t\t} else if !iInf && !jInf {\n482\t\t\t\t\tswap = cells[j].GDOP > cells[i].GDOP\n483\t\t\t\t}\n484\t\n485\t\t\t\tif swap {\n486\t\t\t\t\tcells[i], cells[j] = cells[j], cells[i]\n487\t\t\t\t}\n488\t\t\t}\n489\t\t}\n490\t\n491\t\t// Return top N worst cells\n492\t\tif n > len(cells) {\n493\t\t\tn = len(cells)\n494\t\t}\n495\t\treturn cells[:n]\n496\t}\n497\t\n498\t// GetBestCoverageCells returns the N cells with the best GDOP values\n499\tfunc (gc *GDOPComputer) GetBestCoverageCells(results [][]GDOPResult, n int) []GDOPResult {\n500\t\tif len(results) == 0 {\n501\t\t\treturn nil\n502\t\t}\n503\t\n504\t\t// Flatten all cells\n505\t\tcells := make([]GDOPResult, 0)\n506\t\tfor _, row := range results {\n507\t\t\tcells = append(cells, row...)\n508\t\t}\n509\t\n510\t\t// Sort by GDOP (ascending, so best first)\n511\t\tfor i := 0; i < len(cells); i++ {\n512\t\t\tfor j := i + 1; j < len(cells); j++ {\n513\t\t\t\t// Handle infinity: finite values are better than infinity\n514\t\t\t\tiInf := math.IsInf(cells[i].GDOP, 0)\n515\t\t\t\tjInf := math.IsInf(cells[j].GDOP, 0)\n516\t\n517\t\t\t\tvar swap bool\n518\t\t\t\tif !iInf && jInf {\n519\t\t\t\t\tswap = false // i is finite, j is infinity, i is better\n520\t\t\t\t} else if iInf && !jInf {\n521\t\t\t\t\tswap = true // i is infinity, j is finite, j should be before i\n522\t\t\t\t} else if !iInf && !jInf {\n523\t\t\t\t\tswap = cells[j].GDOP < cells[i].GDOP\n524\t\t\t\t}\n525\t\n526\t\t\t\tif swap {\n527\t\t\t\t\tcells[i], cells[j] = cells[j], cells[i]\n528\t\t\t\t}\n529\t\t\t}\n530\t\t}\n531\t\n532\t\t// Return top N best cells\n533\t\tif n > len(cells) {\n534\t\t\tn = len(cells)\n535\t\t}\n536\t\treturn cells[:n]\n537\t}\n538\t\n539\t// OptimizeNodePositions uses a greedy algorithm to find better node positions\n540\t// for a given number of nodes within the space\n541\tfunc OptimizeNodePositions(space *Space, numNodes int, iterations int) *NodeSet {\n542\t\tminX, minY, _, maxX, maxY, maxZ := space.Bounds()\n543\t\n544\t\t// Start with corner positions\n545\t\tbestSet := NewNodeSet()\n546\t\tcorners := CornerPositions(space)\n547\t\n548\t\tfor i := 0; i < numNodes; i++ {\n549\t\t\tif i < len(corners) {\n550\t\t\t\tbestSet.AddVirtualNode(\n551\t\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"node-%d\", i),\n552\t\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"Node %d\", i+1),\n553\t\t\t\t\tcorners[i],\n554\t\t\t\t)\n555\t\t\t} else {\n556\t\t\t\t// Add random position (Go 1.20+ automatically seeds global generator)\n557\t\t\t\tpos := Point{\n558\t\t\t\t\tX: minX + mrand.Float64()*(maxX-minX),\n559\t\t\t\t\tY: minY + mrand.Float64()*(maxY-minY),\n560\t\t\t\t\tZ: mrand.Float64() * maxZ,\n561\t\t\t\t}\n562\t\t\t\tbestSet.AddVirtualNode(\n563\t\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"node-%d\", i),\n564\t\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"Node %d\", i+1),\n565\t\t\t\t\tpos,\n566\t\t\t\t)\n567\t\t\t}\n568\t\t}\n569\t\n570\t\t// Generate initial links and compute coverage\n571\t\tlinks := GenerateAllLinks(bestSet)\n572\t\tgdopComp := NewGDOPComputer(links, GridConfig{\n573\t\t\tMinX:     minX,\n574\t\t\tMinY:     minY,\n575\t\t\tWidth:    maxX - minX,\n576\t\t\tDepth:    maxY - minY,\n577\t\t\tCellSize: 0.2,\n578\t\t})\n579\t\tresults := gdopComp.ComputeAll()\n580\t\tbestScore := gdopComp.CoverageScore(results)\n581\t\n582\t\t// Iterative improvement\n583\t\tfor iter := 0; iter < iterations; iter++ {\n584\t\t\t// Try moving each node slightly\n585\t\t\tfor i, node := range bestSet.All() {\n586\t\t\t\t// Generate candidate position\n587\t\t\t\tcandidatePos := Point{\n588\t\t\t\t\tX: node.Position.X + (mrand.Float64()-0.5)*1.0, // ±0.5m\n589\t\t\t\t\tY: node.Position.Y + (mrand.Float64()-0.5)*1.0,\n590\t\t\t\t\tZ: node.Position.Z + (mrand.Float64()-0.5)*0.5, // Less Z variation\n591\t\t\t\t}\n592\t\n593\t\t\t\t// Keep within bounds\n594\t\t\t\tcandidatePos.X = math.Max(minX, math.Min(maxX, candidatePos.X))\n595\t\t\t\tcandidatePos.Y = math.Max(minY, math.Min(maxY, candidatePos.Y))\n596\t\t\t\tcandidatePos.Z = math.Max(0, math.Min(maxZ, candidatePos.Z))\n597\t\n598\t\t\t\t// Create test set with this node moved\n599\t\t\t\ttestSet := NewNodeSet()\n600\t\t\t\tfor j, n := range bestSet.All() {\n601\t\t\t\t\tif j == i {\n602\t\t\t\t\t\ttestSet.AddVirtualNode(n.ID, n.Name, candidatePos)\n603\t\t\t\t\t} else {\n604\t\t\t\t\t\ttestSet.Add(n)\n605\t\t\t\t\t}\n606\t\t\t\t}\n607\t\n608\t\t\t\t// Evaluate\n609\t\t\t\ttestLinks := GenerateAllLinks(testSet)\n610\t\t\t\ttestGDOP := NewGDOPComputer(testLinks, GridConfig{\n611\t\t\t\t\tMinX:     minX,\n612\t\t\t\t\tMinY:     minY,\n613\t\t\t\t\tWidth:    maxX - minX,\n614\t\t\t\t\tDepth:    maxY - minY,\n615\t\t\t\t\tCellSize: 0.2,\n616\t\t\t\t})\n617\t\t\t\ttestResults := testGDOP.ComputeAll()\n618\t\t\t\ttestScore := testGDOP.CoverageScore(testResults)\n619\t\n620\t\t\t\t// Keep if better\n621\t\t\t\tif testScore > bestScore {\n622\t\t\t\t\tbestScore = testScore\n623\t\t\t\t\tbestSet.All()[i].Position = candidatePos\n624\t\t\t\t}\n625\t\t\t}\n626\t\t}\n627\t\n628\t\treturn bestSet\n629\t}\n630\t\n631\t// GenerateShoppingList creates a shopping list from simulation results\n632\tfunc GenerateShoppingList(space *Space, currentNodes *NodeSet) *ShoppingList {\n633\t\tnodes := currentNodes\n634\t\tif nodes == nil || nodes.Count() == 0 {\n635\t\t\tnodes = SuggestedNodes(space, 4)\n636\t\t}\n637\t\n638\t\tlinks := GenerateAllLinks(nodes)\n639\t\tminX, minY, _, maxX, maxY, _ := space.Bounds()\n640\t\n641\t\tgdopComp := NewGDOPComputer(links, GridConfig{\n642\t\t\tMinX:     minX,\n643\t\t\tMinY:     minY,\n644\t\t\tWidth:    maxX - minX,\n645\t\t\tDepth:    maxY - minY,\n646\t\t\tCellSize: 0.2,\n647\t\t})\n648\t\tresults := gdopComp.ComputeAll()\n649\t\n650\t\tcoverage := gdopComp.CoverageScore(results)\n651\t\tavgGDOP := gdopComp.AverageGDOP(results)\n652\t\n653\t\treturn &ShoppingList{\n654\t\t\tMinimumNodes:     MinimumNodeCount(space, 4.0),\n655\t\t\tRecommendedNodes: nodes.Count(),\n656\t\t\tExpectedAccuracy: ExpectedAccuracy(avgGDOP),\n657\t\t\tCoveragePercent:  coverage,\n658\t\t\tOptimalPositions: extractNodePositions(nodes),\n659\t\t}\n660\t}\n661\t\n662\tfunc extractNodePositions(nodes *NodeSet) []Point {\n663\t\tpositions := make([]Point, 0, nodes.Count())\n664\t\tfor _, n := range nodes.All() {\n665\t\t\tpositions = append(positions, n.Position)\n666\t\t}\n667\t\treturn positions\n668\t}\n669\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"f4b14098-bcf4-41f7-b291-a83ac1750e10","uuid":"e5dc6016-29c5-4cf9-ad04-dc8b81deff9a","timestamp":"2026-05-06T03:12:55.925Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/coding/spaxel/mothership/internal/simulator/gdop.go","content":"package simulator\n\nimport (\n\t\"fmt\"\n\t\"math\"\n\tmrand \"math/rand\"\n)\n\n// GDOPResult contains GDOP computation results for a single cell\ntype GDOPResult struct {\n\tX, Y, Z      float64  // Cell center position\n\tGDOP         float64  // Computed GDOP value (Infinity = no coverage)\n\tQuality      string   // \"excellent\", \"good\", \"fair\", \"poor\", \"none\"\n\tContributingLinks []string // Link IDs that contributed to this cell\n}\n\n// GridConfig defines the GDOP computation grid\ntype GridConfig struct {\n\tCellSize    float64 // Grid cell size in meters\n\tMinX, MinY  float64 // Grid origin\n\tWidth       float64 // Grid width\n\tDepth       float64 // Grid depth\n}\n\n// GDOPComputer computes Geometric Dilution of Precision for coverage analysis\ntype GDOPComputer struct {\n\tlinks     []Link\n\tconfig    GridConfig\n\tmaxZone   int // Maximum Fresnel zone to consider (default 3)\n}\n\n// NewGDOPComputer creates a new GDOP computer\nfunc NewGDOPComputer(links []Link, config GridConfig) *GDOPComputer {\n\tif config.CellSize <= 0 {\n\t\tconfig.CellSize = 0.2 // Default 20cm\n\t}\n\treturn &GDOPComputer{\n\t\tlinks:   links,\n\t\tconfig:  config,\n\t\tmaxZone: 3, // Default: consider first 3 Fresnel zones\n\t}\n}\n\n// SetMaxZone sets the maximum Fresnel zone to consider\nfunc (gc *GDOPComputer) SetMaxZone(zone int) {\n\tif zone < 1 {\n\t\tzone = 1\n\t}\n\tgc.maxZone = zone\n}\n\n// ComputeAll computes GDOP for the entire grid\n// Returns a slice of GDOP results indexed by cell position\nfunc (gc *GDOPComputer) ComputeAll() [][]GDOPResult {\n\tnx := int(math.Ceil(gc.config.Width / gc.config.CellSize))\n\tny := int(math.Ceil(gc.config.Depth / gc.config.CellSize))\n\n\tresults := make([][]GDOPResult, ny)\n\n\tfor iy := 0; iy < ny; iy++ {\n\t\tresults[iy] = make([]GDOPResult, nx)\n\t\tfor ix := 0; ix < nx; ix++ {\n\t\t\tx := gc.config.MinX + (float64(ix)+0.5)*gc.config.CellSize\n\t\t\ty := gc.config.MinY + (float64(iy)+0.5)*gc.config.CellSize\n\t\t\tz := 1.0 // Use 1m height for 2D GDOP analysis\n\n\t\t\tresult := gc.ComputeAt(x, y, z)\n\t\t\tresults[iy][ix] = result\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn results\n}\n\n// ComputeAt computes GDOP at a specific point\nfunc (gc *GDOPComputer) ComputeAt(x, y, z float64) GDOPResult {\n\tpoint := Point{X: x, Y: y, Z: z}\n\n\t// Collect links that cover this point (within maxZone Fresnel zones)\n\tvar coveringLinks []Link\n\tvar linkIDs []string\n\n\tfor _, link := range gc.links {\n\t\tif IsInFresnelZones(link.TX.Position, link.RX.Position, point, gc.maxZone) {\n\t\t\tcoveringLinks = append(coveringLinks, link)\n\t\t\tlinkIDs = append(linkIDs, link.TX.ID+\":\"+link.RX.ID)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tresult := GDOPResult{\n\t\tX:                 x,\n\t\tY:                 y,\n\t\tZ:                 z,\n\t\tContributingLinks: linkIDs,\n\t}\n\n\tif len(coveringLinks) < 2 {\n\t\t// Need at least 2 links for 2D localization\n\t\tresult.GDOP = math.Inf(1)\n\t\tresult.Quality = \"none\"\n\t\treturn result\n\t}\n\n\t// Compute GDOP using angular diversity\n\tgdop := gc.computeGDOPAngular(point, coveringLinks)\n\tresult.GDOP = gdop\n\tresult.Quality = gdopToQuality(gdop)\n\n\treturn result\n}\n\n// computeGDOPAngular computes GDOP based on angular diversity of link directions\n// This is the 2D GDOP formula from the plan\nfunc (gc *GDOPComputer) computeGDOPAngular(point Point, links []Link) float64 {\n\t// Step 1: Collect link angles\n\t// For each link, compute the angle of the line from TX to RX as seen from point\n\ttype linkAngle struct {\n\t\ttheta float64 // angle in radians\n\t\tlink  Link\n\t}\n\n\tangles := make([]linkAngle, 0, len(links))\n\tfor _, link := range links {\n\t\t// Project to floor plane (ignore Z for 2D analysis)\n\t\tdx := link.RX.Position.X - link.TX.Position.X\n\t\tdy := link.RX.Position.Y - link.TX.Position.Y\n\t\ttheta := math.Atan2(dy, dx)\n\t\tangles = append(angles, linkAngle{theta: theta, link: link})\n\t}\n\n\tif len(angles) < 2 {\n\t\treturn math.Inf(1)\n\t}\n\n\t// Step 2: Build Fisher information matrix\n\t// F = Σ [ [cos²(θ),       cos(θ)·sin(θ)],\n\t//        [cos(θ)·sin(θ), sin²(θ)       ] ]\n\tvar f00, f01, f11 float64\n\n\tfor _, la := range angles {\n\t\tc := math.Cos(la.theta)\n\t\ts := math.Sin(la.theta)\n\n\t\tf00 += c * c\n\t\tf01 += c * s\n\t\tf11 += s * s\n\t}\n\n\t// Step 3: Compute determinant\n\tdet := f00*f11 - f01*f01\n\n\t// Check for degenerate geometry (collinear links)\n\tif det <= 1e-6 {\n\t\treturn math.Inf(1)\n\t}\n\n\t// Step 4: Compute trace of F^-1\n\t// For 2x2 matrix: trace(F^-1) = (f00 + f11) / det\n\ttraceFInv := (f00 + f11) / det\n\n\t// Step 5: GDOP = sqrt(trace(F^-1))\n\tgdop := math.Sqrt(traceFInv)\n\n\treturn gdop\n}\n\n// gdopToQuality converts GDOP value to quality string\nfunc gdopToQuality(gdop float64) string {\n\tif math.IsInf(gdop, 0) {\n\t\treturn \"none\"\n\t}\n\tif gdop < 2.0 {\n\t\treturn \"excellent\"\n\t}\n\tif gdop < 4.0 {\n\t\treturn \"good\"\n\t}\n\tif gdop < 8.0 {\n\t\treturn \"fair\"\n\t}\n\treturn \"poor\"\n}\n\n// CoverageScore computes the percentage of cells with \"good\" or better coverage\nfunc (gc *GDOPComputer) CoverageScore(results [][]GDOPResult) float64 {\n\tif len(results) == 0 || len(results[0]) == 0 {\n\t\treturn 0\n\t}\n\n\tgoodCells := 0\n\ttotalCells := 0\n\n\tfor _, row := range results {\n\t\tfor _, cell := range row {\n\t\t\ttotalCells++\n\t\t\tif cell.Quality == \"excellent\" || cell.Quality == \"good\" {\n\t\t\t\tgoodCells++\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn 100.0 * float64(goodCells) / float64(totalCells)\n}\n\n// AverageGDOP computes the average GDOP over all cells (excluding infinity)\nfunc (gc *GDOPComputer) AverageGDOP(results [][]GDOPResult) float64 {\n\tsum := 0.0\n\tcount := 0\n\n\tfor _, row := range results {\n\t\tfor _, cell := range row {\n\t\t\tif !math.IsInf(cell.GDOP, 0) {\n\t\t\t\tsum += cell.GDOP\n\t\t\t\tcount++\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tif count == 0 {\n\t\treturn math.Inf(1)\n\t}\n\treturn sum / float64(count)\n}\n\n// QualityCounts returns the count of cells by quality level\nfunc (gc *GDOPComputer) QualityCounts(results [][]GDOPResult) map[string]int {\n\tcounts := map[string]int{\n\t\t\"excellent\": 0,\n\t\t\"good\":      0,\n\t\t\"fair\":      0,\n\t\t\"poor\":      0,\n\t\t\"none\":      0,\n\t}\n\n\tfor _, row := range results {\n\t\tfor _, cell := range row {\n\t\t\tcounts[cell.Quality]++\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn counts\n}\n\n// FindDeadZones returns positions where coverage is \"none\" or \"poor\"\nfunc (gc *GDOPComputer) FindDeadZones(results [][]GDOPResult) []Point {\n\tdeadZones := make([]Point, 0)\n\n\tfor _, row := range results {\n\t\tfor _, cell := range row {\n\t\t\tif cell.Quality == \"none\" || cell.Quality == \"poor\" {\n\t\t\t\tdeadZones = append(deadZones, Point{X: cell.X, Y: cell.Y, Z: cell.Z})\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn deadZones\n}\n\n// RecommendNodePosition suggests optimal positions for additional nodes\n// based on current dead zones\nfunc (gc *GDOPComputer) RecommendNodePosition(results [][]GDOPResult, space *Space) Point {\n\t// Find the centroid of the largest dead zone\n\tdeadZones := gc.FindDeadZones(results)\n\tif len(deadZones) == 0 {\n\t\t// No dead zones, suggest center of space\n\t\tminX, minY, _, maxX, maxY, _ := space.Bounds()\n\t\treturn Point{\n\t\t\tX: (minX + maxX) / 2,\n\t\t\tY: (minY + maxY) / 2,\n\t\t\tZ: 2.0, // Suggest high placement\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Cluster dead zones and find the largest cluster\n\t// Simplified: just return the centroid of all dead zones\n\tvar sumX, sumY float64\n\tfor _, dz := range deadZones {\n\t\tsumX += dz.X\n\t\tsumY += dz.Y\n\t}\n\n\tcentroid := Point{\n\t\tX: sumX / float64(len(deadZones)),\n\t\tY: sumY / float64(len(deadZones)),\n\t\tZ: 2.0, // Suggest high placement for better coverage\n\t}\n\n\treturn centroid\n}\n\n// MinimumNodeCount estimates the minimum number of nodes needed for good coverage\n// Based on space dimensions and desired quality threshold\nfunc MinimumNodeCount(space *Space, targetGDOP float64) int {\n\twidth, depth, _ := space.Dimensions()\n\tarea := width * depth\n\n\t// Heuristic: nodes needed based on area and desired GDOP\n\t// For GDOP < 4 (good coverage): approximately 1 node per 15-20 m²\n\tif targetGDOP < 2 {\n\t\t// Excellent coverage requires more nodes\n\t\treturn int(math.Ceil(area / 15.0))\n\t}\n\tif targetGDOP < 4 {\n\t\t// Good coverage\n\t\treturn int(math.Ceil(area / 20.0))\n\t}\n\t// Fair coverage\n\treturn int(math.Ceil(area / 30.0))\n}\n\n// ExpectedAccuracy estimates the expected localization accuracy at a point\n// based on its GDOP value\nfunc ExpectedAccuracy(gdop float64) float64 {\n\tif math.IsInf(gdop, 0) {\n\t\treturn math.Inf(1)\n\t}\n\n\t// Based on research: typical CSI accuracy with 4+ nodes is ±0.5-1.0m\n\t// GDOP < 2: ±0.5m, GDOP 2-4: ±1.0m, GDOP > 4: degrades further\n\tbaseAccuracy := 0.5 // meters for GDOP = 1\n\n\treturn baseAccuracy * gdop\n}\n\n// GDOPColor represents a color for GDOP visualization\ntype GDOPColor struct {\n\tR, G, B uint8 // RGB values 0-255\n}\n\n// GDOPColorMap returns the color for a given GDOP value for visualization\n// Uses: green (excellent), yellow (good), orange (fair), red (poor), gray (none)\nfunc GDOPColorMap(gdop float64) GDOPColor {\n\tif math.IsInf(gdop, 0) {\n\t\treturn GDOPColor{R: 80, G: 80, B: 80} // Gray for no coverage\n\t}\n\tif gdop < 2.0 {\n\t\treturn GDOPColor{R: 34, G: 197, B: 94} // Green (#22c65e) for excellent\n\t}\n\tif gdop < 4.0 {\n\t\treturn GDOPColor{R: 255, G: 193, B: 7} // Yellow (#ffc107) for good\n\t}\n\tif gdop < 8.0 {\n\t\treturn GDOPColor{R: 255, G: 146, B: 0} // Orange (#ff9200) for fair\n\t}\n\treturn GDOPColor{R: 220, G: 53, B: 69} // Red (#dc3545) for poor\n}\n\n// GDOPHeatmapData represents flattened GDOP data for frontend rendering\ntype GDOPHeatmapData struct {\n\tWidth      int         `json:\"width\"`       // Grid width (columns)\n\tDepth      int         `json:\"depth\"`       // Grid depth (rows)\n\tCellSize   float64     `json:\"cell_size\"`   // Cell size in meters\n\tOriginX    float64     `json:\"origin_x\"`    // Grid origin X\n\tOriginY    float64     `json:\"origin_y\"`    // Grid origin Y\n\tGDOPValues []float64   `json:\"gdop_values\"` // Flattened GDOP values (9999 = infinity)\n\tQualities  []string    `json:\"qualities\"`   // Flattened quality strings\n\tColors     [][]uint8   `json:\"colors\"`      // Flattened RGB colors [width*depth*3]\n\tAccuracyMap []float64   `json:\"accuracy_map\"` // Expected accuracy in meters per cell\n}\n\n// ToHeatmapData converts GDOP results to a heatmap-friendly format\nfunc (gc *GDOPComputer) ToHeatmapData(results [][]GDOPResult) *GDOPHeatmapData {\n\tif len(results) == 0 || len(results[0]) == 0 {\n\t\treturn &GDOPHeatmapData{}\n\t}\n\n\tdepth := len(results)    // rows (Y)\n\twidth := len(results[0]) // cols (X)\n\ttotalCells := width * depth\n\n\tdata := &GDOPHeatmapData{\n\t\tWidth:       width,\n\t\tDepth:       depth,\n\t\tCellSize:    gc.config.CellSize,\n\t\tOriginX:     gc.config.MinX,\n\t\tOriginY:     gc.config.MinY,\n\t\tGDOPValues:  make([]float64, totalCells),\n\t\tQualities:   make([]string, totalCells),\n\t\tColors:      make([][]uint8, totalCells),\n\t\tAccuracyMap: make([]float64, totalCells),\n\t}\n\n\tfor y := 0; y < depth; y++ {\n\t\tfor x := 0; x < width; x++ {\n\t\t\tidx := y*width + x\n\t\t\tresult := results[y][x]\n\n\t\t\t// GDOP value (9999 for infinity)\n\t\t\tif math.IsInf(result.GDOP, 0) {\n\t\t\t\tdata.GDOPValues[idx] = 9999.0\n\t\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t\tdata.GDOPValues[idx] = result.GDOP\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t// Quality string\n\t\t\tdata.Qualities[idx] = result.Quality\n\n\t\t\t// RGB color\n\t\t\tcolor := GDOPColorMap(result.GDOP)\n\t\t\tdata.Colors[idx] = []uint8{color.R, color.G, color.B}\n\n\t\t\t// Expected accuracy (use 9999.0 for infinity)\n\t\t\taccuracy := ExpectedAccuracy(result.GDOP)\n\t\t\tif math.IsInf(accuracy, 0) {\n\t\t\t\tdata.AccuracyMap[idx] = 9999.0\n\t\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t\tdata.AccuracyMap[idx] = accuracy\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn data\n}\n\n// ComputeAccuracyMap computes expected accuracy for each cell\n// Returns a 2D array of accuracy values in meters (infinity = no coverage)\nfunc (gc *GDOPComputer) ComputeAccuracyMap(results [][]GDOPResult) [][]float64 {\n\tif len(results) == 0 {\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\n\taccuracyMap := make([][]float64, len(results))\n\tfor i := range results {\n\t\taccuracyMap[i] = make([]float64, len(results[i]))\n\t\tfor j := range results[i] {\n\t\t\taccuracyMap[i][j] = ExpectedAccuracy(results[i][j].GDOP)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn accuracyMap\n}\n\n// ComputeColorMap computes RGB colors for each cell for visualization\n// Returns a flattened array of RGB values [width*depth*3]\nfunc (gc *GDOPComputer) ComputeColorMap(results [][]GDOPResult) [][]uint8 {\n\tif len(results) == 0 || len(results[0]) == 0 {\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\n\tdepth := len(results)\n\twidth := len(results[0])\n\ttotalCells := width * depth\n\n\tcolors := make([][]uint8, totalCells)\n\n\tfor y := 0; y < depth; y++ {\n\t\tfor x := 0; x < width; x++ {\n\t\t\tidx := y*width + x\n\t\t\tcolor := GDOPColorMap(results[y][x].GDOP)\n\t\t\tcolors[idx] = []uint8{color.R, color.G, color.B}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn colors\n}\n\n// GetWorstCoverageCells returns the N cells with the worst GDOP values\nfunc (gc *GDOPComputer) GetWorstCoverageCells(results [][]GDOPResult, n int) []GDOPResult {\n\tif len(results) == 0 {\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\n\t// Flatten all cells\n\tcells := make([]GDOPResult, 0)\n\tfor _, row := range results {\n\t\tcells = append(cells, row...)\n\t}\n\n\t// Sort by GDOP (descending, so worst first)\n\tfor i := 0; i < len(cells); i++ {\n\t\tfor j := i + 1; j < len(cells); j++ {\n\t\t\t// Handle infinity: infinity is worse than any finite value\n\t\t\tiInf := math.IsInf(cells[i].GDOP, 0)\n\t\t\tjInf := math.IsInf(cells[j].GDOP, 0)\n\n\t\t\tvar swap bool\n\t\t\tif iInf && !jInf {\n\t\t\t\tswap = false // i stays (infinity at top)\n\t\t\t} else if !iInf && jInf {\n\t\t\t\tswap = true // j is infinity, should be before i\n\t\t\t} else if !iInf && !jInf {\n\t\t\t\tswap = cells[j].GDOP > cells[i].GDOP\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\tif swap {\n\t\t\t\tcells[i], cells[j] = cells[j], cells[i]\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Return top N worst cells\n\tif n > len(cells) {\n\t\tn = len(cells)\n\t}\n\treturn cells[:n]\n}\n\n// GetBestCoverageCells returns the N cells with the best GDOP values\nfunc (gc *GDOPComputer) GetBestCoverageCells(results [][]GDOPResult, n int) []GDOPResult {\n\tif len(results) == 0 {\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\n\t// Flatten all cells\n\tcells := make([]GDOPResult, 0)\n\tfor _, row := range results {\n\t\tcells = append(cells, row...)\n\t}\n\n\t// Sort by GDOP (ascending, so best first)\n\tfor i := 0; i < len(cells); i++ {\n\t\tfor j := i + 1; j < len(cells); j++ {\n\t\t\t// Handle infinity: finite values are better than infinity\n\t\t\tiInf := math.IsInf(cells[i].GDOP, 0)\n\t\t\tjInf := math.IsInf(cells[j].GDOP, 0)\n\n\t\t\tvar swap bool\n\t\t\tif !iInf && jInf {\n\t\t\t\tswap = false // i is finite, j is infinity, i is better\n\t\t\t} else if iInf && !jInf {\n\t\t\t\tswap = true // i is infinity, j is finite, j should be before i\n\t\t\t} else if !iInf && !jInf {\n\t\t\t\tswap = cells[j].GDOP < cells[i].GDOP\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\tif swap {\n\t\t\t\tcells[i], cells[j] = cells[j], cells[i]\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Return top N best cells\n\tif n > len(cells) {\n\t\tn = len(cells)\n\t}\n\treturn cells[:n]\n}\n\n// OptimizeNodePositions uses a greedy algorithm to find better node positions\n// for a given number of nodes within the space\nfunc OptimizeNodePositions(space *Space, numNodes int, iterations int) *NodeSet {\n\tminX, minY, _, maxX, maxY, maxZ := space.Bounds()\n\n\t// Start with corner positions\n\tbestSet := NewNodeSet()\n\tcorners := CornerPositions(space)\n\n\tfor i := 0; i < numNodes; i++ {\n\t\tif i < len(corners) {\n\t\t\tbestSet.AddVirtualNode(\n\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"node-%d\", i),\n\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"Node %d\", i+1),\n\t\t\t\tcorners[i],\n\t\t\t)\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t// Add random position (Go 1.20+ automatically seeds global generator)\n\t\t\tpos := Point{\n\t\t\t\tX: minX + mrand.Float64()*(maxX-minX),\n\t\t\t\tY: minY + mrand.Float64()*(maxY-minY),\n\t\t\t\tZ: mrand.Float64() * maxZ,\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tbestSet.AddVirtualNode(\n\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"node-%d\", i),\n\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"Node %d\", i+1),\n\t\t\t\tpos,\n\t\t\t)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Generate initial links and compute coverage\n\tlinks := GenerateAllLinks(bestSet)\n\tgdopComp := NewGDOPComputer(links, GridConfig{\n\t\tMinX:     minX,\n\t\tMinY:     minY,\n\t\tWidth:    maxX - minX,\n\t\tDepth:    maxY - minY,\n\t\tCellSize: 0.2,\n\t})\n\tresults := gdopComp.ComputeAll()\n\tbestScore := gdopComp.CoverageScore(results)\n\n\t// Iterative improvement\n\tfor iter := 0; iter < iterations; iter++ {\n\t\t// Try moving each node slightly\n\t\tfor i, node := range bestSet.All() {\n\t\t\t// Generate candidate position\n\t\t\tcandidatePos := Point{\n\t\t\t\tX: node.Position.X + (mrand.Float64()-0.5)*1.0, // ±0.5m\n\t\t\t\tY: node.Position.Y + (mrand.Float64()-0.5)*1.0,\n\t\t\t\tZ: node.Position.Z + (mrand.Float64()-0.5)*0.5, // Less Z variation\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t// Keep within bounds\n\t\t\tcandidatePos.X = math.Max(minX, math.Min(maxX, candidatePos.X))\n\t\t\tcandidatePos.Y = math.Max(minY, math.Min(maxY, candidatePos.Y))\n\t\t\tcandidatePos.Z = math.Max(0, math.Min(maxZ, candidatePos.Z))\n\n\t\t\t// Create test set with this node moved\n\t\t\ttestSet := NewNodeSet()\n\t\t\tfor j, n := range bestSet.All() {\n\t\t\t\tif j == i {\n\t\t\t\t\ttestSet.AddVirtualNode(n.ID, n.Name, candidatePos)\n\t\t\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t\t\ttestSet.Add(n)\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t// Evaluate\n\t\t\ttestLinks := GenerateAllLinks(testSet)\n\t\t\ttestGDOP := NewGDOPComputer(testLinks, GridConfig{\n\t\t\t\tMinX:     minX,\n\t\t\t\tMinY:     minY,\n\t\t\t\tWidth:    maxX - minX,\n\t\t\t\tDepth:    maxY - minY,\n\t\t\t\tCellSize: 0.2,\n\t\t\t})\n\t\t\ttestResults := testGDOP.ComputeAll()\n\t\t\ttestScore := testGDOP.CoverageScore(testResults)\n\n\t\t\t// Keep if better\n\t\t\tif testScore > bestScore {\n\t\t\t\tbestScore = testScore\n\t\t\t\tbestSet.All()[i].Position = candidatePos\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn bestSet\n}\n\n// GenerateShoppingList creates a shopping list from simulation results\nfunc GenerateShoppingList(space *Space, currentNodes *NodeSet) *ShoppingList {\n\tnodes := currentNodes\n\tif nodes == nil || nodes.Count() == 0 {\n\t\tnodes = SuggestedNodes(space, 4)\n\t}\n\n\tlinks := GenerateAllLinks(nodes)\n\tminX, minY, _, maxX, maxY, _ := space.Bounds()\n\n\tgdopComp := NewGDOPComputer(links, GridConfig{\n\t\tMinX:     minX,\n\t\tMinY:     minY,\n\t\tWidth:    maxX - minX,\n\t\tDepth:    maxY - minY,\n\t\tCellSize: 0.2,\n\t})\n\tresults := gdopComp.ComputeAll()\n\n\tcoverage := gdopComp.CoverageScore(results)\n\tavgGDOP := gdopComp.AverageGDOP(results)\n\n\treturn &ShoppingList{\n\t\tMinimumNodes:     MinimumNodeCount(space, 4.0),\n\t\tRecommendedNodes: nodes.Count(),\n\t\tExpectedAccuracy: ExpectedAccuracy(avgGDOP),\n\t\tCoveragePercent:  coverage,\n\t\tOptimalPositions: extractNodePositions(nodes),\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc extractNodePositions(nodes *NodeSet) []Point {\n\tpositions := make([]Point, 0, nodes.Count())\n\tfor _, n := range nodes.All() {\n\t\tpositions = append(positions, n.Position)\n\t}\n\treturn positions\n}\n","numLines":669,"startLine":1,"totalLines":669}}}

Compute(walkers []*SimWalker, blobs []BlobResult) AccuracyReport {\n49\t\tif len(walkers) == 0 {\n50\t\t\treturn AccuracyReport{}\n51\t\t}\n52\t\n53\t\t// Collect all true positions and matched blob positions\n54\t\ttruePositions := make([]Point, 0)\n55\t\tdetectedPositions := make([]Point, 0)\n56\t\terrors := make([]float64, 0)\n57\t\n58\t\tfor _, walker := range walkers {\n59\t\t\tfor _, truePos := range walker.TrueHistory {\n60\t\t\t\ttruePositions = append(truePositions, truePos)\n61\t\n62\t\t\t\t// Find nearest blob\n63\t\t\t\tnearestDist := math.Inf(1)\n64\t\t\t\tfor _, blob := append(errors, nearestDist)\n76\t\t\t\t}\n77\t\t\t}\n78\t\t}\n79\t\n80\t\tif len(errors) == 0 {\n81\t\t\treturn AccuracyReport{\n82\t\t\t\tMedianError:    math.Inf(1),\n83\t\t\t\tMeanError:      math.Inf(1),\n84\t\t\t\tMaxError:       math.Inf(1),\n85\t\t\t\tDetectionRate:  0,\n86\t\t\t\tSampleCount:    len(truePositions),\n87\t\t\t}\n88\t\t}\n89\t\n90\t\t// Compute statistics\n91\t\tmeanError := 0.0\n92\t\tfor _, e := range errors {\n93\t\t\tmeanError += e\n94\t\t}\n95\t\tmeanError /= float64(len(errors))\n96\t\n97\t\t// Median error\n98\t\tsortedErrors := make([]float64, len(errors))\n99\t\tcopy(sortedErrors, errors)\n100\t\tfor i := 0; i < len(sortedErrors); i++ {\n101\t\t\tfor j := i + 1; j < len(sortedErrors); j++ {\n102\t\t\t\tif sortedErrors[i] > sortedErrors[len(sortedErrors)/2]\n108\t\n109\t\t// Max error\n110\t\tmaxError := sortedErrors[len(sortedErrors)-1]\n111\t\n112\t\t// 95th percentile\n113\t\tp95Index := int(float64(len(sortedErrors)) * 0.95)\n114\t\tif p95Index >= len(sortedErrors) {\n115\t\t\tp95Index = len(sortedErrors) - 1\n116\t\t}\n117\t\tp95Error := sortedErrors[p95Index]\n118\t\n119\t\t// Detection rate\n120\t\tdetectionRate := float64(len(detectedPositions)) / float64(len(truePositions))\n121\t\n122\t\t// Recall at 1m and 2m\n123\t\trecall1m := 0.0\n124\t\trecall2m := 0.0\n125\t\tfor _, e := range errors {\n126\t\t\tif e <= 1.0 {\n127\t\t\t\trecall1m++\n128\t\t\t}\n129\t\t\tif e <= 2.0 {\n130\t\t\t\trecall2m++\n131\t\t\t}\n132\t\t}\n133\t\trecall1m /= float64(len(errors))\n134\t\trecall2m /= float64(len(errors))\n135\t\n136\t\t// False positive rate (blobs without matching walker)\n137\t\tfalsePositives := 0\n138\t\tfor _, blob := range blobs {\n139\t\t\thasMatch := false\n140\t\t\tfor {\n147\t\t\t\tfalsePositives++\n148\t\t\t}\n149\t\t}\n150\t\tfalsePositiveRate := float64(falsePositives) / float64(len(errors))\n151\t\n152\t\treturn AccuracyReport{\n153\t\t\tMedianError:       medianError,\n154\t\t\tMeanError:         meanError,\n155\t\t\tMaxError:          maxError,\n156\t\t\tP95Error:          p95Error,\n157\t\t\tDetectionRate:
